{"key_event":"Election of 1800 and Jeffersonian retrenchment","reasoning":"Organized parties, partisan newspapers, the peaceful transfer of power, broader immigrant naturalization, and judicial checks strengthened white male civic mobilization, though slavery’s expansion, Native dispossession, and the embargo’s disruption limited the gain. Federal authority gained judicial independence and territorial reach through Louisiana, but Jeffersonian tax and military cuts plus weak embargo compliance left national coercive and fiscal capacity slightly below the Federalist-era peak.","society_change":0.3,"society_power":6.8,"state_change":-0.2,"state_power":4.8}
